original_prompt:
/autopilot Role: Senior Frontend Developer

Context: In our Next.js application, the components/ExportPDF.js file is currently hardcoding the US Dollar ($) symbol in the formatNum helper function. This is causing Indian stocks (like TATAPOWER.NS) to display $ instead of ₹ in the generated PDF's data tables and DCF projections, even though the getCurrencySymbol utility is correctly identifying the currency.

Objective: Refactor the formatNum function to accept a dynamic currency symbol parameter, and update all calls to formatNum to pass the dynamically generated symbol.

Action Items:

Locate the formatNum(n) function at the top of ExportPDF.js.

Update its signature to formatNum(n, sym = '$').

Replace all hardcoded '$' + strings inside formatNum with sym +.

Inside the ExportPDF component, locate the sym variable (const sym = getCurrencySymbol(stockData.currency)).

Update all invocations of formatNum() throughout the file to pass sym as the second argument (e.g., formatNum(stockData.marketCap, sym)).

Specifically ensure the DCF 5-Year Projections section (Year ${p.year}: Revenue ${formatNum(p.revenue)}...) is updated to pass the sym argument.

Strict Rule: Do not change any of the jsPDF layout math, coordinates, or page-checking logic. Only touch the formatNum utility and its invocations.

If you want to fix it manually yourself:
It only takes about 30 seconds. Open components/ExportPDF.js and make these two small changes:

1. Change the helper function at the very top of the file:

JavaScript
// BEFORE:
function formatNum(n) {
  if (n == null || isNaN(n)) return '—'
  if (Math.abs(n) >= 1e12) return '$' + (n / 1e12).toFixed(2) + 'T'
  if (Math.abs(n) >= 1e9) return '$' + (n / 1e9).toFixed(2) + 'B'
  if (Math.abs(n) >= 1e6) return '$' + (n / 1e6).toFixed(2) + 'M'
  return '$' + n.toFixed(2)
}

// AFTER:
function formatNum(n, sym = '$') {
  if (n == null || isNaN(n)) return '—'
  if (Math.abs(n) >= 1e12) return sym + (n / 1e12).toFixed(2) + 'T'
  if (Math.abs(n) >= 1e9) return sym + (n / 1e9).toFixed(2) + 'B'
  if (Math.abs(n) >= 1e6) return sym + (n / 1e6).toFixed(2) + 'M'
  return sym + n.toFixed(2)
}
2. Pass the sym variable where formatNum is called:
Search through the file for formatNum( and simply add , sym to the end of the arguments.

For example, the stats array changes to:

JavaScript
['Market Cap', formatNum(stockData.marketCap, sym)],
// ...
['Revenue', formatNum(stockData.revenue, sym)],
['FCF', formatNum(stockData.freeCashFlow, sym)],
And the DCF projections string changes to:

JavaScript
`Year ${p.year}: Revenue ${formatNum(p.revenue, sym)} · EBITDA ${formatNum(p.ebitda
